actions.label: Executive Health Retreat
actions.description: A week at a luxury wellness retreat — bloodwork panels, IV therapy, personal chef, guided recovery. The reset button for people who can afford to press it.
actions.narrative: Seven days of silence, supplements, and sleep. You came back a different person. Your team noticed before you said anything.

actions.label: Join a Concierge Medical Practice
actions.description: Pay a retainer for a doctor who actually knows your name. Same-day appointments, proactive health monitoring, direct cell phone access.
actions.narrative: Your doctor texted you — texted — about your lab results. 'Everything looks great, but let's adjust your vitamin D.' This is what healthcare is supposed to feel like.

actions.label: Hire a Personal Trainer
actions.description: Three sessions a week with someone who won't let you skip. Structured training, accountability, results.
actions.narrative: The trainer doesn't care about your quarterly revenue. She cares about your deadlift form. For three hours a week, you're not a CEO — you're an athlete.

actions.label: Start a Gym Routine
actions.description: Three days a week. Nothing heroic — just showing up consistently. Your energy and focus improve.
actions.narrative: You haven't missed a workout in three weeks. You're sleeping better. Thinking clearer. Funny how moving your body fixes your brain.

actions.label: Optimize Your Sleep
actions.description: Blackout curtains, no screens after 9, consistent bedtime. The cheapest performance upgrade that exists.
actions.narrative: You're sleeping seven hours now instead of five. The difference isn't subtle — your decision-making is sharper, your patience is longer, and that afternoon crash is gone.

actions.label: Build a Morning Routine
actions.description: Wake up early. Journal, move, plan the day before the day plans you. Discipline before the inbox.
actions.narrative: 5:30 AM. Coffee, journal, twenty minutes of movement, then the three most important tasks before anyone else is awake. You own the morning now.

actions.label: Get a Full Physical
actions.description: Blood work, stress test, the works. Know your numbers before they surprise you.
actions.narrative: The doctor said everything looks good — except your cortisol. 'You're running hot,' she said. 'Dial it back before your body does it for you.'

actions.label: Luxury Family Vacation
actions.description: First class, five-star, no budget. Show your family what you've been building this for. Two weeks of undivided presence.
actions.narrative: Your kid looked at you across the dinner table in Santorini and said, 'This is the best trip ever.' You realized it wasn't the hotel — it was that you left your laptop at home.

actions.label: Couples Retreat Weekend
actions.description: A guided weekend away with your partner — structured conversations, reconnection exercises, no distractions.
actions.narrative: You said things you'd been holding for months. So did she. It wasn't easy — but by Sunday night you were holding hands again.

actions.label: Family Trip
actions.description: A real vacation. Not a 'working from the hotel' trip. Phone off, out of office on.
actions.narrative: Your kid asked if you could do this 'every month.' You can't. But the fact that they asked means you've been gone too long.

actions.label: Weekly Date Night
actions.description: Put the phone away. Be present with the person who's been watching you disappear into work.
actions.narrative: She laughed at dinner — really laughed — for the first time in months. You realized you'd missed the sound.

actions.label: Bucket List Experience
actions.description: Skydiving, racing a supercar, private cooking class with a Michelin chef — pick the thing you always said 'someday' about.
actions.narrative: You jumped out of a plane at 14,000 feet. For thirty seconds, nothing existed but wind and sky and the absurd joy of being alive.

actions.label: Fly Private
actions.description: Charter — or fractionally own — a private jet. No security lines, no layovers, no wasted days. The ultimate time-and-energy purchase the truly wealthy make.
actions.narrative: Wheels up forty minutes after you decided to go. You made the meeting, the recital, and dinner at home — same day. Time is the one asset you can't make more of, so you stopped wasting it in terminals.

actions.label: Charter a Superyacht
actions.description: A week on a crewed superyacht in the Med — family, closest friends, a chef, and nowhere to be. The kind of trip that becomes the story your kids tell for decades.
actions.narrative: Anchored off a quiet cove, phones in a drawer, your family laughing on the deck at sunset. You realized this — not the revenue chart — was the entire point.

actions.label: Buy a Private Estate Retreat
actions.description: A second home on land that takes your breath away — a place that's only ever joy, never work. Somewhere the whole family gathers for generations.
actions.narrative: The deed has your name on it, but you already think of it as the family's place. The kids picked their rooms. This is what the work was for.

actions.label: Elite Workshop or Masterclass
actions.description: A weekend intensive with a world-class expert — photography, wine, leadership. Learning at the highest level.
actions.narrative: The instructor was a former CEO turned artist. The other attendees were the kind of people you want in your network. You learned to paint — and picked up two warm leads.

actions.label: Solo Adventure
actions.description: Go somewhere you've never been. Alone. Remember what it feels like to be a person, not a role.
actions.narrative: Standing on a mountain in another state, phone dead, watching the sun go down. Your business is still running. You might be okay.

actions.label: Learn Something New
actions.description: Take a class in something that has nothing to do with your business. Cooking, music, a language.
actions.narrative: Your pottery is terrible. You love it. For two hours a week, you're a beginner again — and it's the most relaxed you've been in months.

actions.label: Spiritual Pilgrimage
actions.description: Walk the Camino, visit a sacred site, or take a solo journey to a place that pulls at your soul. Not a vacation — a reckoning.
actions.narrative: You walked for twelve days. By the end, the blisters didn't matter. Something shifted — not in your business plan, but in your relationship with why you're building at all.

actions.label: Silent Meditation Retreat
actions.description: Ten days. No phone, no talking, no distractions. Just you and the noise in your head — until the noise stops.
actions.narrative: By day three you wanted to leave. By day seven you didn't want it to end. You came back quieter — and that quiet became the most productive force in your life.

actions.label: Join a Faith Community
actions.description: Find a church, temple, sangha, or circle. Belong to something bigger than your P&L statement.
actions.narrative: The pastor asked how business was going. You said 'hard.' He said 'good — that means you're growing.' Simple words. They landed.

actions.label: Start a Meditation Practice
actions.description: Ten minutes a day. Sit with the noise in your head instead of running from it.
actions.narrative: You sat still for ten minutes today without reaching for your phone. It was the hardest and easiest thing you've done all month.

actions.label: Launch a Charitable Foundation
actions.description: Set up a donor-advised fund or small foundation. Give strategically, build legacy, and get the tax benefits.
actions.narrative: The foundation is established. Your name is on something that will outlast your business. The first grant went to a scholarship fund — the kid's thank-you letter is framed on your wall.

actions.label: Endow a Major Foundation
actions.description: Fund a serious, named foundation with professional staff and a real endowment — the kind that moves the needle on a cause and defines a family's public legacy for generations.
actions.narrative: The foundation made its first major grant this week — a seven-figure commitment to a cause you've cared about for years. Your name is now attached to something that will outlive you, and do good the whole time.

actions.label: Make a Major Donation
actions.description: Write a five-figure check to a cause that defines what you stand for.
actions.narrative: The hospital wing dedication had your name on a small plaque. You didn't do it for the plaque — but your kids saw it.

actions.label: Charitable Giving
actions.description: Write a check to a cause that matters. Not for the tax deduction — for the reminder that money is a tool, not a scoreboard.
actions.narrative: The director of the food bank sent a handwritten thank-you note. It's pinned above your desk now.

actions.label: Volunteer Your Time
actions.description: Spend a Saturday building houses, mentoring kids, or serving meals.
actions.narrative: You spent six hours building a wheelchair ramp for a stranger. Your back hurts. Your heart doesn't.

actions.label: Write Your Book
actions.description: Hire a ghostwriter or lock yourself in a cabin. Document what you've learned — the failures, the wins, the real story.
actions.narrative: The manuscript is done. 47,000 words of everything you wish someone had told you. The publisher said yes.

actions.label: Build an Art Collection
actions.description: Acquire serious art — pieces you love that also appreciate, pass to your heirs, and can be donated for tax advantage. Beauty on the walls, value on the balance sheet.
actions.narrative: The first major piece arrived and went up over the fireplace. Your advisor calls it an appreciating asset; you just call it the thing you stop to look at every morning.

actions.label: Establish Family Wealth Council
actions.description: Monthly family meetings about money, values, and the future. Teach your kids what school won't.
actions.narrative: Your teenager asked about compound interest at dinner. Your partner brought up charitable giving goals. This is generational wealth — the conversations.

actions.label: Estate Planning Session
actions.description: Meet with an estate attorney. Make sure what you're building survives you and benefits the people you love.
actions.narrative: Signing the will felt strange — like acknowledging mortality while you're in the middle of building. But your family is protected now.

actions.label: Mentor a Young Entrepreneur
actions.description: Find someone two years behind you. Share what you've learned. Teaching clarifies your own thinking.
actions.narrative: Your mentee landed her first client this month. She texted you at 11 PM. You smiled when you saw it in the morning.

actions.label: Start Therapy or Coaching
actions.description: Talk to someone who isn't your spouse, your partner, or your mirror. Process the stress before it processes you.
actions.narrative: You said things out loud you've been carrying silently for months. The therapist didn't fix anything — but naming it made it lighter.

actions.label: Take the Team on a Company Retreat
actions.description: Fly the whole team somewhere great — work sessions in the morning, real connection the rest of the time. People who feel valued and bonded stop creating drama and start covering for each other.
actions.narrative: By the second night, the org-chart silos were gone — just people who actually like working together. The pettiness and politics that were brewing? Defused. Culture is the cheapest insurance against people problems.

actions.label: Dial In Your Diet & Nutrition
actions.description: Work with a nutritionist and chef-prepared meals built around your bloodwork. Stop running your body on gas-station fuel.
actions.narrative: Real food, dialed to your body. The 3pm crash is gone, your labs improved, and you're leaner than you've been in years — without thinking about it.

actions.label: Executive Leadership Coaching
actions.description: A seasoned coach who's run bigger companies than yours. Sharpen how you think, decide, and lead — the operating system behind everything else.
actions.narrative: Your coach asked one question that reframed a decision you'd been agonizing over for weeks. You lead differently now — calmer, clearer, more deliberate.

actions.label: Buy Your Dream Car
actions.description: The car you taped to your wall as a kid. Not an investment, not a write-off — a pure, unapologetic reward for the years of grind.
actions.narrative: You sat in the driver's seat in the dealership lot for a full minute before turning the key. Some desires you satisfy not because they're smart — but because you earned the right to.

actions.label: Take a Sabbatical Around the World
actions.description: Three months. Hand the keys to your team and go see the world — slowly, deliberately, with no agenda but wonder.
actions.narrative: You came home changed. The business survived without you — which proved you built something real — and you returned with a head full of horizons and a tank that's finally full.

actions.label: Build Your Dream Home
actions.description: The house you design from the ground up — every room how you imagined it. The physical proof of the life you set out to build.
actions.narrative: You walked through the finished house with your family at golden hour. Every detail was a choice you got to make. This is what the years of work were quietly aiming at the whole time.
